title: Why China's Oil Troubles Are Panamax Owners' Gain
section_path: Why China's Oil Troubles Are Panamax Owners' Gain
section_path_text: Why China's Oil Troubles Are Panamax Owners' Gain
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
After reaching a high for the year in mid-May, the Baltic Exchange's panamax index spent a month facing significant headwinds. However, a partial recovery has seen the gauge gain around ten per cent over the past month, putting it about sixteen per cent above the reading at the same time last year. Robust cargo volumes across the panamax and post-panamax segments in the first half of the year contributed to the strong performance.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
By Ulf Bergman
After reaching a high for the year in mid-May, the Baltic Exchange's panamax index spent a month facing significant headwinds. However, a partial recovery has seen the gauge gain around ten per cent over the past month, putting it about sixteen per cent above the reading at the same time last year.

children.title: Coal Supported Global Panamax Volumes
children.summary: According to data from Signal Ocean, 721 million tonnes of dry bulk commodities were shipped onboard panamax and post-panamax vessels over the past six months. Coal accounted for 56 per cent of total volumes, slightly above the average over the past few years.

children.title: Chinese Demand the Key Driver for Seaborne Panamax Volumes
section_path: Chinese Demand the Key Driver for Seaborne Panamax Volumes
children.section_path_text: Chinese Demand the Key Driver for Seaborne Panamax Volumes
children.summary: Changes in Chinese demand accounted for much of the recent ups and downs in panamax volumes. The past two months' year-on-year growth in coal exports on board panamax vessels has largely been driven by greater demand for the fossil fuel from Chinese importers, following significant disruptions to the country's crude oil imports from the Middle East.

children.title: Second Half Volumes to Remain Exposed to Chinese Developments
section_path: Second Half Volumes to Remain Exposed to Chinese Developments
children.section_path_text: Second Half Volumes to Remain Exposed to Chinese Developments
children.summary: Panamax cargo volumes are typically higher in the second half of the year than in the preceding six months. Over the past three years, the increase has ranged from six to thirteen per cent, with the coal trade driving much of the higher demand for seaborne transportation.
